query_info.description: The query info object holds information related to data access within a datastore. To access, manipulate, delete, or retrieve data from a datastore, a database query must be written using a specific syntax.

udid.description: The Apple assigned Unique Device Identifier (UDID). For iOS, iPadOS, tvOS, watchOS and visionOS devices, this is the UDID. For macOS devices, it is the Provisioning UDID. For example: 00008020-008D4548007B4F26

vendor_attributes.description:
The Vendor Attributes object can be used to represent values of attributes populated by the Vendor/Finding Provider. It can help distinguish between the vendor-provided values and consumer-updated values, of key attributes like severity_id.
The original finding producer should not populate this object. It should be populated by consuming systems that support data mutability.
vendor_attributes.caption: Vendor Attributes

is_src_dst_assignment_known.description: true denotes that src_endpoint and dst_endpoint correctly identify the initiator and responder respectively. false denotes that the event source has arbitrarily assigned one peer to src_endpoint and the other to dst_endpoint, in other words that initiator and responder are not being asserted. This can occur, for example, when the event source is a network appliance that has not observed the initiation of a given connection. In the absence of this attribute, interpretation of the initiator and responder is implementation-specific.

7.description: A custom action was executed such as running of a command script. Use the message attribute of the base class for details.
7.caption: Custom Action

8.description: A request or submission was approved. For example, when a form was properly filled out and submitted. This is distinct from 1 'Allowed'.
8.caption: Approved

9.description: A quarantined file or other content was restored to its original location.
9.caption: Restored

10.description: A suspicious or risky entity was deemed to no longer be suspicious (re-scored).
10.caption: Exonerated

11.description: A corrupt file or configuration was corrected.
11.caption: Corrected

12.description: A corrupt file or configuration was partially corrected.
12.caption: Partially Corrected

14.description: An operation was delayed, for example if a restart was required to finish the operation.
14.caption: Delayed

15.description: Suspicious activity or a policy violation was detected without further action.
15.caption: Detected

16.description: The outcome of an operation had no action taken.
16.caption: No Action

17.description: The operation or action was logged without further action.
17.caption: Logged

18.description: A file or other entity was marked with extended attributes.
18.caption: Tagged

20.description: Counted the request or activity but did not determine whether to allow it or block it.
20.caption: Count

21.description: The request was detected as a threat and resulted in the connection being reset.
21.caption: Reset

22.description: Required the end user to solve a CAPTCHA puzzle to prove that a human being is sending the request.
22.caption: Captcha

23.description: Ran a silent challenge that required the client session to verify that it's a browser, and not a bot.
23.caption: Challenge

24.description: The requestor's access has been revoked due to security policy enforcements. Note: use the Host profile if the User or Actor requestor is not present in the event class.
24.caption: Access Revoked

25.description: A request or submission was rejected. For example, when a form was improperly filled out and submitted. This is distinct from 2 'Blocked'.
25.caption: Rejected

26.description: An attempt to access a resource was denied due to an authorization check that failed. This is a more specific disposition than 2 'Blocked' and can be complemented with the authorizations attribute for more detail.
26.caption: Unauthorized

27.description: An error occurred during the processing of the activity or request. Use the message attribute of the base class for details.
27.caption: Error

13.description: A corrupt file or configuration was not corrected.
13.caption: Uncorrected

19.description: The request or activity was detected as a threat and resulted in a notification but request was not blocked.
19.caption: Alert

access_type.caption: Access Type

class.description: The class name of the object. See specific usage.
class.caption: Class

Geohash of the geo-coordinates (latitude and longitude).

Geohashing is a geocoding system used to encode geographic coordinates in decimal degrees, to a single string.

num_resolutions.description: The number of items that were resolved.

start_time.description: The start time of a time period. See specific usage.

token.description: The Token object is the base object for representing tokens, API keys, and authentication credentials. This object provides common attributes for all token types and can be used directly in API activity events to represent API tokens (type_id: 7) or client tokens (type_id: 6) such as Okta API tokens or Microsoft Entra ID Application Registration client secrets. The base token object is also extended by authentication_token for protocol-specific authentication tokens in authentication events. Note: Use authentication_token (which extends token) for protocol-specific authentication tokens (Kerberos, OIDC, SAML) in authentication events, and programmatic_credential for tracking credential lifecycle and usage patterns.
token.caption: Token

subnet_uid.description: The unique identifier of a virtual subnet.
subnet_uid.caption: Subnet UID

access_level.description: The access level of an entity. See specific usage.

99.caption: Other

url.description: The URL object that pertains to the event or object. See specific usage.

This evaluates what permissions an identity has been granted through attached policies, which privileges are actively used versus unused, and identifies potential over-privileged access. Use this for identity-centric security assessments such as privilege audits, dormant permission discovery, and least-privilege compliance analysis.

For example, this can encapsulate os_signals from CrowdStrike Falcon Zero Trust Assessments, or account for Datastore configurations from Cyera.

The time when the logging system collected and logged the event.

This attribute is distinct from the event time in that event time typically contain the time extracted from the original event. Most of the time, these two times will be different.

3.description: The socket has passively received a connection request from a remote peer.
3.caption: SYN-RECEIVED

6.description: The socket connection has been closed by the local application, the remote peer has closed its half of the connection, and the system is waiting to be sure that the remote peer received the last acknowledgement.
6.caption: TIME-WAIT

1.description: The socket has an established connection between a local application and a remote peer.
1.caption: ESTABLISHED

2.description: The socket is actively trying to establish a connection to a remote peer.
2.caption: SYN-SENT

4.description: The socket connection has been closed by the local application, the remote peer has not yet acknowledged the close, and the system is waiting for it to close its half of the connection.
4.caption: FIN-WAIT-1

5.description: The socket connection has been closed by the local application, the remote peer has acknowledged the close, and the system is waiting for it to close its half of the connection.
5.caption: FIN-WAIT-2

7.description: The socket is not in use.
7.caption: CLOSED

8.description: The socket connection has been closed by the remote peer, and the system is waiting for the local application to close its half of the connection.
8.caption: CLOSE-WAIT

9.description: The socket connection has been closed by the remote peer, the local application has closed its half of the connection, and the system is waiting for the remote peer to acknowledge the close.
9.caption: LAST-ACK

10.description: The socket is listening for incoming connections.
10.caption: LISTEN

11.description: The socket connection has been closed by the local application and the remote peer simultaneously, and the remote peer has not yet acknowledged the close attempt of the local application.
11.caption: CLOSING

Examples include network taps, span ports, inline security devices, or packet capture systems.

Indicates whether the credentials were passed in clear text.

For cloud connections, this translates to the traffic-boundary(same VPC, through IGW, etc.). For traditional networks, this is described as Local, Internal, or External.
boundary.caption: Boundary

lang.description: The two letter lower case language codes, as defined by ISO 639-1. For example: en (English), de (German), or fr (French).
lang.caption: Language

type_uid.description: The event/finding type ID. It identifies the event's semantics and structure. The value is calculated by the logging system as: class_uid * 100 + activity_id.
type_uid.caption: Type ID

meets_criteria.description: Determines if an assessment, control, policy, or otherwise meets its assessment criteria. See specific usage.
meets_criteria.caption: Meets Criteria

length.description: The HTTP response length, in number of bytes.
length.caption: Response Length

deleted_time_dt.description: The timestamp when the user was deleted. In Active Directory (AD), when a user is deleted they are moved to a temporary container and then removed after 30 days. So, this field can be populated even after a user is deleted for the next 30 days.
deleted_time_dt.caption: Deleted Time

original_event_uid.description: The unique identifier assigned to the event in its original logging system before transformation to OCSF format. This field preserves the source system's native event identifier, enabling traceability back to the raw log entry. For example, a Windows Event Record ID, a syslog message ID, a Splunk _cd value, or a database transaction log sequence number.
original_event_uid.caption: Original Event ID

access_mask.description: The access mask in a platform-native format.
access_mask.caption: Access Mask

http_status.description: The Hypertext Transfer Protocol (HTTP) status code returned to the client.
http_status.caption: HTTP Status

transmit_time_dt.description: The event transmission time from one device to another.  See specific usage.
transmit_time_dt.caption: Transmission Time

is_applied.description: A determination if a policy, rule, or enforcement action was applied.
is_applied.caption: Applied

relay.description: The network relay that is associated with the event.
relay.caption: Relay

access_analysis_result.description: Describes access relationships and pathways between identities, resources, focusing on who can access what and through which mechanisms. This evaluates access levels (read/write/admin), access types (direct, cross-account, public, federated), and the conditions under which access is granted. Use this for resource-centric security assessments such as external access discovery, public exposure analysis, etc.
access_analysis_result.caption: Access Analysis Result

cloud_partition.description: The logical grouping or isolated segment within a cloud provider's infrastructure. See specific usage.
cloud_partition.caption: Cloud Partition

proxy_traffic.description: The network traffic refers to the amount of data moving across a network, from proxy to remote server at a given point of time.
proxy_traffic.caption: Proxy Traffic

ja3_hash.description: The MD5 hash of a JA3 string.
ja3_hash.caption: JA3 Hash

entity.description: The managed entity that is being acted upon.
entity.caption: Entity

locations.description: A list of detailed geographical locations.
locations.caption: Geo Locations

is_locked.description: Indicates if the entity is locked. See specific usage.
is_locked.caption: Locked

injection_type.description: The process injection method, normalized to the caption of the injection_type_id value. In the case of 'Other', it is defined by the event source.
injection_type.caption: Injection Type

standards.description: Compliance standards are a set of criteria organizations can follow to protect sensitive and confidential information. e.g. NIST SP 800-53, CIS AWS Foundations Benchmark v1.4.0, ISO/IEC 27001
standards.caption: Compliance Standards: List

email_addrs.description: A list of additional email addresses for the user.
email_addrs.caption: Email Addresses

data_sources.description: A list of data sources utilized in generation of the finding.
data_sources.caption: Data Sources

trace.description: The information about the trace. See specific usage.
trace.caption: Trace

campaign.description: The campaign object describes details about the campaign that was the source of the activity.
campaign.caption: Campaign

types.description: The type/s of an entity. See specific usage.
types.caption: Types

sender_mailbox.description: The human readable email address of the system or server that actually transmitted the email message, extracted from the email headers per RFC 5322. This differs from the from_mailbox field, which shows the message author. The sender mailbox field is most commonly used when multiple addresses appear in the from_mailboxes field, or when the transmitting system is different from the message author (such as when sending on behalf of someone else).
sender_mailbox.caption: Sender Mailbox

meid.description: The Mobile Equipment Identifier. It's a unique number that identifies a Code Division Multiple Access (CDMA) mobile device.
meid.caption: MEID

tickets.description: The associated ticket(s) in the ticketing system. Each ticket contains details like ticket ID, status, etc.
tickets.caption: Tickets

reply_to_mailboxes.description: The human-readable email header Reply To Mailbox values. For example 'Example User <example.user@usersdomain.com>'.
reply_to_mailboxes.caption: Reply To Mailboxes

risk_score.description: The risk score as reported by the event source.
risk_score.caption: Risk Score

finding.description: The Finding object provides details about a finding/detection generated by a security tool.
finding.caption: Finding

codes.description: The list of numeric responses sent to a request.
codes.caption: Response Codes

job.description: The job object that pertains to the event.
job.caption: Job

is_personal.description: The event occurred on a personal device.
is_personal.caption: Personal Device

criticality.description: Criticality of a resource/object in question
criticality.caption: Criticality

first_seen_time.description: The initial detection time of the activity or object. See specific usage
first_seen_time.caption: First Seen

action.description: The normalized caption of 'action_id' or the source specific action.

unmapped.description: The attributes that are not mapped to the event schema. The names and values of those attributes are specific to the event source.
unmapped.caption: Unmapped Data

sandbox.description: The name of the containment jail (i.e., sandbox). For example, hardened_ps, high_security_ps, oracle_ps, netsvcs_ps, or default_ps.

role.description: The role of an entity in the context of the event or finding, normalized to the caption of the role_id value. In the case of 'Other', it is defined by the event source. See specific usage.

execute_count.description: The execute count. See specific usage.
execute_count.caption: Execute Count

dkim.description: The DomainKeys Identified Mail (DKIM) status of the email.
dkim.caption: DKIM Status

run_state.description: The state of the job or service, normalized to the caption of the run_state_id value. In the case of 'Other', it is defined by the event source. See specific usage.
run_state.caption: Run State

referrer.description: The request header that identifies the address of the previous web page, which is linked to the current web page or resource being requested.

authorizations.description: Provides details about an authorization, such as authorization outcome, and any associated policies related to the activity/event.

parent_process.description: The parent process of this process object. It is recommended to only populate this field for the top-level process object, to prevent deep nesting. Additional ancestry information can be supplied in the ancestry attribute.

cis_controls.caption: CIS Controls

schedule_uid.description: The unique identifier of the schedule associated with a scan job.
schedule_uid.caption: Schedule UID

profiles.caption: Profiles

long.description: The geographical Longitude coordinate represented in Decimal Degrees (DD). For example: -71.057083.

identifier_cookie.description: The client identifier cookie during client/server exchange.

cpu_cores.description: The number of processor cores in all installed processors. For Example: 42.

created_time_dt.description: The time when the object was created. See specific usage.
created_time_dt.caption: Created Time

autonomous_system.description: The Autonomous System details associated with an IP address.
autonomous_system.caption: Autonomous System

vendor_id_list.description: The list of vendor IDs. See specific usage.
vendor_id_list.caption: Vendor ID List

data_classifications.description: A list of Data Classification objects, that include information about data classification levels and data category types, identified by a classifier.
data_classifications.caption: Data Classification

completion_tokens.description: Number of tokens in the model's response/completion for this message.
completion_tokens.caption: Completion Tokens

fixed_in_version.description: The software package version in which a reported vulnerability was patched/fixed.
fixed_in_version.caption: Fixed In Version

transformation_info_list.description: An array of transformation info that describes the mappings or transforms applied to the data.
transformation_info_list.caption: Transformation Info

is_vpn.description: The indication of whether the session is a VPN session.
is_vpn.caption: VPN Session

version.description: The version that pertains to the event or object. See specific usage.
version.caption: Version

to_mailboxes.description: The human-readable email header To Mailbox values. For example 'Example User <example.user@usersdomain.com>'.
to_mailboxes.caption: To Mailboxes

is_default.description: The indication of whether the value is from a default value name. For example, the value name could be missing.
is_default.caption: Default Value

confidence_score.description: The confidence score as reported by the event source.
confidence_score.caption: Confidence Score

applications.description: A list of application objects. See specific usage.
applications.caption: Applications

unmanned_aerial_system.description: The Unmanned Aerial System object describes the characteristics, Position Location Information (PLI), and other metadata of Unmanned Aerial Systems (UAS) and other unmanned and drone systems used in Remote ID. Remote ID is defined in the Standard Specification for Remote ID and Tracking (ASTM Designation: F3411-22a) ASTM F3411-22a.
unmanned_aerial_system.caption: Unmanned Aerial System

end_time_dt.description: The end time of a time period. See specific usage.
end_time_dt.caption: End Time

tags.description: The list of tags; {key:value} pairs attached to an entity. A Tag may be used to categorize, filter, and search for entities. For e.g. "Environment": "Production", "Owner": "007". See specific usage.
tags.caption: Tags

is_truncated.description: Indicates that an attribute has been truncated. See specific usage.
is_truncated.caption: Is Truncated

ja4_fingerprint_list.description: A list of the JA4+ network fingerprints.
ja4_fingerprint_list.caption: JA4+ Fingerprints

graph.description: A graph data structure representation with nodes and edges.
graph.caption: Graph

num_violations.description: The number of times the policy or rule was violated.
num_violations.caption: Violations

accessed_time_dt.description: The time when the file was last accessed.

agent.description: An Agent (also known as a Sensor) is typically installed on an Operating System (OS) and serves as a specialized software component that can be designed to monitor, detect, collect, archive, or take action. These activities and possible actions are defined by the upstream system controlling the Agent and its intended purpose. For instance, an Agent can include Endpoint Detection & Response (EDR) agents, backup/disaster recovery sensors, Application Performance Monitoring or profiling sensors, and similar software.
agent.caption: Agent

from_mailboxes.description: The human-readable email header From Mailbox values. This array should contain the value in from_mailbox. For example 'Example User <example.user@usersdomain.com>'.
from_mailboxes.caption: From Mailboxes

3.description: The data was being processed, accessed, or read by a system, making it active in memory or CPU. E.g., sensitive data in a Business Intelligence tool, ePHI being processed in an EHR application or a user viewing data stored in a spreadsheet or PDF.
3.caption: Data in-Use

0.description: The data lifecycle state is unknown.
0.caption: Unknown

1.description: The data was stored on physical or logical media and was not actively moving through the network nor was being processed. E.g., data stored in a database, PDF files in a file share, or EHR records in object storage.
1.caption: Data at-Rest

2.description: The data was actively moving through the network or from one physical or logical location to another. E.g., emails being send, data replication or Change Data Capture (CDC) streams, or sensitive data processed on an API.

99.description: The data lifecycle state is not mapped. See the data_lifecycle_state attribute, which contains a data source specific value.

is_user_provisioning_enabled.description: Indicates whether user provisioning is automated (e.g., for a SCIM resource). See specific usage.

bus_type.description: The attachment bus or interface standard, normalized to the caption of the bus_type_id value. In the case of 'Other', it is defined by the event source.

1.description: A kernel mode driver.
1.caption: Kernel Mode

2.description: A user mode service.
2.caption: User Mode

99.description: The service category is not mapped. See the service_category attribute, which contains an event source specific value.
99.caption: Other

win/service_category_id.caption: Service Category ID

uploaded_time.description: The timestamp at which an entity was uploaded. See specific usage.
uploaded_time.caption: Uploaded Time

function_name.caption: Function Name

precision.description: The numeric precision. See specific usage.
precision.caption: Precision

query_type.description: The normalized caption of query_type_id or the source-specific query type.
query_type.caption: Query Type

status_details.description: A list of descriptions, containing additional information about the event/finding status.

It standardizes user and group provisioning details, enabling identity synchronization and lifecycle management with compatible Identity Providers (IdPs) and applications. SCIM is defined in RFC-7634

volume.description: The volume on the storage device where the file is located. See specific usage.

path.description: The path that pertains to the event or object. See specific usage.

column_name.description: The name of the column. See specific usage.

idle_timeout.description: Duration (in minutes) of allowed inactivity before a timeout See specific usage.

source.description: The source, when used with source_id, is normalized to the caption of the source_id value. In the case of 'Other', it is defined by the event source. See specific usage.

value.description: The value associated to an attribute. See specific usage.

column_number.description: The number of the column. See specific usage.

surname.description: The last or family name for the user.

vram_size.description: The total amount of installed video RAM.

activity_name.description: The event activity name, as defined by the activity_id.
activity_name.caption: Activity

dkim_signature.description: The DomainKeys Identified Mail (DKIM) signature used by the sending/receiving system.

exploit_last_seen_time.description: The time when the exploit was most recently observed.

processed_time_dt.description: The event processed time, such as an ETL operation.

modifier.description: The user that last modified the object associated with the event. See specific usage.

category_id.description: The normalized identifier of the object category. See specific usage.

3.description: The drive is a remote (network) drive.

account.description: The account object describes details about the account that was the source or target of the activity. See specific usage.

number.description: The number of the entity. See specific usage.

This object is inclusive of both the basic and Enterprise User Schema Extension.

status.description: The event status, normalized to the caption of the status_id value. In the case of 'Other', it is defined by the event source.

condition_keys.description: The list of condition keys and their values that were evaluated as part of a rule or policy. Condition keys are used to define circumstances under which the rule/policy is in effect. See specific usage.
condition_keys.caption: Condition Keys

original_time.description: The original event time as reported by the event source. For example, the time in the original format from system event log such as Syslog on Unix/Linux and the System event file on Windows. Omit if event is generated instead of collected via logs.
original_time.caption: Original Time

ip.description: The IP address, in either IPv4 or IPv6 format.
ip.caption: IP Address

raw_data.description: The raw event/finding data as received from the source.
raw_data.caption: Raw Data

observation_type.description: The classification or category of the observation, indicating what kind of measurement or finding it represents. See specific usage.
observation_type.caption: Observation Type

99.description: The status is not mapped. See the status attribute, which contains a data source specific value.
99.caption: Other

status_id.description: The normalized identifier of the event status.
status_id.caption: Status ID

url_string.description: The URL string. See RFC 1738. For example: http://www.example.com/download/trouble.exe.
url_string.caption: URL String

page_number.description: The page number of the document. See specific usage.
page_number.caption: Page Number

3.description: System calls the user's registered phone number and requires the user to answer and provide a response.
3.caption: Phone Call

6.description: Physical device that generates a code to be used for authentication.
6.caption: Hardware Token

1.description: User receives and inputs a code sent to their mobile device via SMS text message.
1.caption: SMS

2.description: The user responds to a security question as part of a question-based authentication factor
2.caption: Security Question

4.description: Devices that verify identity-based on user's physical identifiers, such as fingerprint scanners or retina scanners.
4.caption: Biometric

5.description: Push notification is sent to user's registered device and requires the user to acknowledge.
5.caption: Push Notification

7.description: Application generates a one-time password (OTP) for use in authentication.
7.caption: OTP

8.description: A code or link is sent to a user's registered email address.
8.caption: Email

9.description: Typically involves a hardware token, which the user physically interacts with to authenticate.
9.caption: U2F

10.description: Web-based API that enables users to register devices as authentication factors.
10.caption: WebAuthn

11.description: The user enters a password that they have previously established.
11.caption: Password

factor_type_id.description: The normalized identifier for the authentication factor.
factor_type_id.caption: Factor Type ID

vram_mode.description: The video memory attachment mode, indicating how the VRAM hardware is integrated with the system (e.g., shared or dedicated), normalized to the caption of the vram_mode_id value. For 'Other', the exact attachment mode is defined by the event source.
vram_mode.caption: VRAM Mode

log_type.description: The log type, normalized to the caption of the log_type_id value. In the case of 'Other', it is defined by the event source.
log_type.caption: Log Type

key_uid.description: The unique identifier of the key. See specific usage.
key_uid.caption: Key UID

is_directed.description: Indicates if the entity has directionality. See specific usage.
is_directed.caption: Directed

time_dt.description: The normalized event occurrence time or the finding creation time.
time_dt.caption: Event Time

exploit_ref_url.description: The URL of the exploit code or Proof-of-Concept (PoC).
exploit_ref_url.caption: Exploit URL

is_unused.description: Indicates whether an item is unused. See specific usage.
is_unused.caption: Is Unused

duration_months.description: Represents the duration of the activity in months. See specific usage.
duration_months.caption: Duration Months

fix_available.description: Indicates if a fix is available for the reported vulnerability.
fix_available.caption: Fix Availability

network_interfaces.description:
The physical or virtual network interfaces that are associated with the device, one for each unique MAC address/IP address/hostname/name combination.

Note: The first element of the array is the network information that pertains to the event.
network_interfaces.caption: Network Interfaces

digest.description: The message digest attribute contains the fixed length message hash representation and the corresponding hashing algorithm information.
digest.caption: Message Digest

3.description: A user mode service started automatically during system startup.
3.caption: Auto

0.description: The service start type is unknown.
0.caption: Unknown

1.description: A kernel mode driver loaded at boot.
1.caption: Boot

2.description: A kernel mode driver loaded during system startup.
2.caption: System

99.description: The service start type is not mapped. See the service_start_type attribute, which contains an event source specific value.
99.caption: Other

4.description: A user mode service started on demand when a process calls StartService.
4.caption: Demand

5.description: A driver or service that cannot be started.
5.caption: Disabled

win/service_start_type_id.description: The normalized identifier of the service start type.
win/service_start_type_id.caption: Service Start Type ID

detection_system.description: The name of the type of data security tool or system that the finding, detection, or alert originated from. E.g., Endpoint, Secure Email Gateway, etc.
detection_system.caption: Detection System

status_detail.description: The status detail contains additional information about the event/finding outcome.
status_detail.caption: Status Detail

span.description: The information about the span. See specific usage.
span.caption: Span

last_run_time_dt.description: The last run time of application or service. See specific usage.
last_run_time_dt.caption: Last Run

pressure_altitude.description: The uncorrected barometric pressure altitude (based on reference standard 29.92 inHg, 1013.25 mb) provides a reference for algorithms that utilize 'altitude deltas' between aircraft. This value is provided in meters and must have a minimum resolution of 1 m.. Special Values: Invalid, No Value, or Unknown: -1000 m.
pressure_altitude.caption: Pressure Altitude

component.description: The component of a data object. See specific usage.
component.caption: Component

certificate.description: The certificate object containing information about the digital certificate.
certificate.caption: Certificate

isp_org.description: The organization name of the Internet Service Provider (ISP). This represents the parent organization or company that owns/operates the ISP. For example, Comcast Corporation would be the ISP org for Xfinity internet service. This attribute helps identify the ultimate provider when ISPs operate under different brand names.
isp_org.caption: ISP Org

proxy.description: The proxy (server) in a network connection.
proxy.caption: Proxy

region.description: The name or the code of a region. See specific usage.
region.caption: Region

cpu_speed.description: The speed of the processor in Mhz. For Example: 4200.
cpu_speed.caption: Processor Speed

duration_weeks.description: Represents the duration of the activity in weeks. See specific usage.
duration_weeks.caption: Duration Weeks

3.caption: PCIe x8

6.description: M.2 (NGFF) internal module form factor/connector exposing PCIe (and/or SATA/USB) for SSDs and add‑in modules.
6.caption: M.2

0.description: The bus type is unknown or not reported.
0.caption: Unknown

1.description: The device is attached directly on the motherboard or SoC (often referred to as 'integrated' or 'onboard').
1.caption: Onboard

2.description: Peripheral Component Interconnect Express slot with 16 lanes, typically used for high‑bandwidth add‑in devices.
2.caption: PCIe x16

99.description: A bus or interface standard not covered by the defined values; the exact value is reported by the event source.
99.caption: Other

4.description: Mobile PCI Express Module (MXM) Type A form factor (compact laptop/embedded GPU module).
4.caption: MXM Type A

5.description: Mobile PCI Express Module (MXM) Type B form factor (larger laptop/embedded GPU module).
5.caption: MXM Type B

7.caption: CXL

bus_type_id.description: The normalized identifier of the attachment bus or interface standard.
bus_type_id.caption: Bus Type ID

duration.description: This represents the duration of the activity in milliseconds. See specific usage.
duration.caption: Duration Milliseconds

vendor_name.description: The name of the vendor. See specific usage.
vendor_name.caption: Vendor Name

location.description: The detailed geographical location usually associated with an IP address.
location.caption: Geo Location

license.description: The name or identifier of the license applied on package or software. See SPDX License List.
license.caption: Software License

file_result.description: The result of the file change. It should contain the new values of the changed attributes.
file_result.caption: File Result

classification.description: The classification as defined by the vendor.
classification.caption: Classification

assignee.description: The details of the user assigned to an Incident.
assignee.caption: Assignee

first_seen_time_dt.description: The initial detection time of the activity or object. See specific usage
first_seen_time_dt.caption: First Seen

code.description: The numeric response sent to a request.
code.caption: Response Code

log_name.description: The log name. See specific usage.
log_name.caption: Log Name

query_evidence.description: The resulting evidence discovered from the evidence search request.
query_evidence.caption: Query Evidence

company_name.description: The name of the company that published the file. For example: Microsoft Corporation.
company_name.caption: Company Name

discovery_details.description: A collection of Discovery Details objects. See specific usage.
discovery_details.caption: Discovery Details

intermediate_ips.description: The intermediate IP Addresses. For example, the IP addresses in the HTTP X-Forwarded-For header.
intermediate_ips.caption: Intermediate IP Addresses

compliance_references.description: A list of reference KB articles that provide information to help organizations understand, interpret, and implement compliance standards. They provide guidance, best practices, and examples.
compliance_references.caption: Compliance Standard References

condition.description: The condition that was evaluated in a rule, policy. See specific usage.
condition.caption: Condition

chassis.description: The chassis type describes the system enclosure or physical form factor. Such as the following examples for Windows Windows Chassis Types
chassis.caption: Chassis

cc.description: The machine-readable email header Cc values, as defined by RFC 5322. For example example.user@usersdomain.com.
cc.caption: Cc

total_potential_attacks_count.description: The total count of privilege-to-attack technique mappings identified across all analyzed privileges.
total_potential_attacks_count.caption: Total Potential Attacks Count

purl.description: A purl is a URL string used to identify and locate a software package in a mostly universal and uniform way across programming languages, package managers, packaging conventions, tools, APIs and databases.
purl.caption: Package URL

file.description: The file that pertains to the event or object. See specific usage.
file.caption: File

from_list.description: The machine-readable email header From values. This array should contain the value in from. For example example.user@usersdomain.com.
from_list.caption: From List

caption.description: A short description or caption of the device. For example: Scanner 1 or Database Manager.
caption.caption: Caption

impact_score.description: The impact as an integer value of the finding, valid range 0-100.
impact_score.caption: Impact Score

user_agent.description: The request header that identifies the operating system and web browser.
user_agent.caption: HTTP User-Agent

severity.description: The event/finding severity, normalized to the caption of the severity_id value. In the case of 'Other', it is defined by the source.
severity.caption: Severity

bytes.description: The total number of bytes (in and out).
bytes.caption: Total Bytes

start_time_dt.description: The start time of a time period. See specific usage.
start_time_dt.caption: Start Time

overall_score.description: The overall score as reported by the event source. See specific usage.
overall_score.caption: Overall Score

web_resources.description: Describes details about web resources that were affected by an activity/event.
web_resources.caption: Web Resources

pid.description: The process identifier, as reported by the operating system. Process ID (PID) is a number used by the operating system to uniquely identify an active process.
pid.caption: Process ID

package_manager.description: The software packager manager utilized to manage a package on a system, e.g. npm, yum, dpkg etc.
package_manager.caption: Package Manager

edition.description: The operating system edition. For example: Professional.
edition.caption: OS Edition

autoscale_uid.description: The unique identifier of the cloud autoscale configuration.
autoscale_uid.caption: Autoscale UID

office_location.description: The primary office location associated with the user. This could be any string and isn't a specific address. For example, South East Virtual.
office_location.caption: Office Location

3.description: Action is required but the situation is not serious at this time.
3.caption: Medium

6.description: An error occurred but it is too late to take remedial action.
6.caption: Fatal

1.description: Informational message. No action required.
1.caption: Informational

2.description: The user decides if action is needed.
2.caption: Low

99.description: The event/finding severity is not mapped. See the severity attribute, which contains a data source specific value.
99.caption: Other

4.description: Action is required immediately.
4.caption: High

5.description: Action is required immediately and the scope is broad.
5.caption: Critical

severity_id.description:
The normalized identifier of the event/finding severity.

The normalized severity is a measurement the effort and expense required to manage and resolve an event or incident. Smaller numerical values represent lower impact events, and larger numerical values represent higher impact events.
severity_id.caption: Severity ID

network_endpoint.description: The Network Endpoint object describes characteristics of a network endpoint. See specific usage.
network_endpoint.caption: Network Endpoint

ldap_dn.description: The X.500 Distinguished Name (DN) is a structured string that uniquely identifies an entry, such as a user, in an X.500 directory service For example, cn=John Doe,ou=People,dc=example,dc=com.
ldap_dn.caption: LDAP Distinguished Name

dictionary.name: dictionary
dictionary.description: The Attribute Dictionary provides a comprehensive catalog of standardized attributes used across OCSF. It defines each attribute's properties, data types, and relationships. This dictionary serves as the authoritative reference for attribute definitions and ensures consistent usage throughout the schema.

json_t.description: Embedded JSON value. A value can be a string, or a number, or true or false or null, or an object or an array. These structures can be nested. See www.json.org.
json_t.caption: JSON

bytestring_t.description: Base 64 encoded immutable byte sequence. Traditional Base 64 is preferred but publishers may use URL-safe Base 64 when known to be acceptable to consumers. These encodings are described in RFC 4648.
bytestring_t.caption: Byte String

datetime_t.description:
The Internet Date/Time format as defined in RFC-3339. For example:
2024-09-10T23:20:50.520Z,
2024-09-10 23:20:50.520789Z.
datetime_t.caption: Datetime

file_hash_t.description:
Fingerprint. A value, in any format, that maps an arbitrarily large data item to a much shorter string that uniquely identifies the original data. Examples include cryptographic hashing of a file, code signing, and Network Protocol Fingerprinting (NPF).

Note about name. The type name file_hast_t and the caption "Hash" are used for legacy reasons. This type has been generalized from a file hash to a general fingerprint. The existing type name and caption were retained for backwards compatibility.
file_hash_t.caption: Hash

file_path_t.description:
The full path to the file. For example: For example:
c:\windows\system32\svchost.exe.
file_path_t.caption: File Path

hostname_t.description:
Unique name assigned to a device connected to a computer network. It may be a fully qualified domain name (FQDN). For example:
r2-d2.example.com.,
mx.example.com
hostname_t.caption: Hostname

ip_t.description:
Internet Protocol address (IP address), in either IPv4 or IPv6 format. For example:
192.168.200.24,
2001:0db8:85a3:0000:0000:8a2e:0370:7334.
ip_t.caption: IP Address

resource_uid_t.description: Resource unique identifier. For example, S3 Bucket name or EC2 Instance ID.
resource_uid_t.caption: Resource UID

subnet_t.description:
The subnet represented in a CIDR notation, using the format network_address/prefix_length. The network_address can be in either IPv4 or IPv6 format. The prefix length indicates the number of bits used for the network portion, and the remaining bits are available for host addresses within that subnet. For example:
192.168.1.0/24,
2001:0db8:85a3:0000::/64
subnet_t.caption: Subnet

timestamp_t.description:
The timestamp format is the number of milliseconds since the Epoch 01/01/1970 00:00:00 UTC. For example:
1618524549901.
timestamp_t.caption: Timestamp

types.description: The data types available in OCSF. Each data type specifies constraints in the form regular expressions, max lengths or value limits. Implementers of OCSF should ensure they abide to these constraints.
types.caption: Data Types

dictionary.caption: Attribute Dictionary

linux.name: linux
linux.description: The Linux extension defines Linux specific attributes, profiles, objects, and classes.
linux.caption: Linux

win.name: win
win.description: The Windows extension defines Windows specific attributes, objects, and classes.
win.caption: Windows

macos.description: The macOS extension defines macOS specific attributes, profiles, objects, and classes.
macos.caption: macOS

data.description: The data returned from the query execution.
data.caption: Data

name.caption: Name

bytes.caption: Total Bytes

uid.caption: Unique ID

query_string.caption: Query String

query_time.caption: Query Time

query_time_dt.description: The time when the query was run.
query_time_dt.caption: Query Time

query_info.description: The query info object holds information related to data access within a datastore. To access, manipulate, delete, or retrieve data from a datastore, a query must be written using a specific syntax.
query_info.caption: Query Information

severity.description: The finding severity, as reported by the Vendor (Finding Provider). The value should be normalized to the caption of the severity_id value. In the case of 'Other', it is defined by the source.
severity.caption: Severity

3.description: Action is required but the situation is not serious at this time.
3.caption: Medium

6.description: An error occurred but it is too late to take remedial action.
6.caption: Fatal

1.description: Informational message. No action required.
1.caption: Informational

2.description: The user decides if action is needed.
2.caption: Low

99.description: The event/finding severity is not mapped. See the severity attribute, which contains a data source specific value.
99.caption: Other

4.description: Action is required immediately.

5.caption: Critical

severity_id.description: The finding severity ID, as reported by the Vendor (Finding Provider).
severity_id.caption: Severity ID

vendor_attributes.name: vendor_attributes
vendor_attributes.description:
The Vendor Attributes object can be used to represent values of attributes populated by the Vendor/Finding Provider. It can help distinguish between the vendor-provided values and consumer-updated values, of key attributes like severity_id.
The original finding producer should not populate this object. It should be populated by consuming systems that support data mutability.
vendor_attributes.caption: Vendor Attributes

name.description: The friendly name of the process, for example: Notepad++.
name.caption: Name

pid.description: The process identifier, as reported by the operating system. Process ID (PID) is a number used by the operating system to uniquely identify an active process.
pid.caption: Process ID

uid.description: A unique identifier for this process assigned by the producer (tool).  Facilitates correlation of a process event with other events for that process.
uid.caption: Unique ID

cmd_line.description: The full command line used to launch an application, service, process, or job. For example: ssh user@10.0.0.10. If the command line is unavailable or missing, the empty string '' is to be used.
cmd_line.caption: Command Line

cpid.description: A unique process identifier that can be assigned deterministically by multiple system data producers.
cpid.caption: Common Process Identifier

created_time.description: The time when the process was created/started.
created_time.caption: Created Time

created_time_dt.description: The time when the process was created/started.
created_time_dt.caption: Created Time

process_entity.name: process_entity
process_entity.description: The Process Entity object provides critical fields for referencing a process.
process_entity.caption: Process Entity

version.description: The CVSS version. For example: 3.1.
version.caption: Version

depth.description: The CVSS depth represents a depth of the equation used to calculate CVSS score.
depth.caption: CVSS Depth

severity.description:
The Common Vulnerability Scoring System (CVSS) Qualitative Severity Rating. A textual representation of the numeric score.

CVSS v2.0
  • Low (0.0 – 3.9)
  • Medium (4.0 – 6.9)
  • High (7.0 – 10.0)

CVSS v3.0
  • None (0.0)
  • Low (0.1 - 3.9)
  • Medium (4.0 - 6.9)
  • High (7.0 - 8.9)
  • Critical (9.0 - 10.0)
severity.caption: Severity

metrics.description: The Common Vulnerability Scoring System metrics. This attribute contains information on the CVE's impact. If the CVE has been analyzed, this attribute will contain any CVSSv2 or CVSSv3 information associated with the vulnerability. For example: { {"Access Vector", "Network"}, {"Access Complexity", "Low"}, ...}.
metrics.caption: Metrics

base_score.description: The CVSS base score. For example: 9.1.
base_score.caption: Base Score

overall_score.description: The CVSS overall score, impacted by base, temporal, and environmental metrics. For example: 9.1.
overall_score.caption: Overall Score

src_url.description: The source URL for the CVSS score. For example: https://nvd.nist.gov/vuln/detail/CVE-2021-44228
src_url.caption: Source URL

vector_string.description: The CVSS vector string is a text representation of a set of CVSS metrics. It is commonly used to record or transfer CVSS metric information in a concise form. For example: 3.1/AV:L/AC:L/PR:L/UI:N/S:U/C:H/I:N/A:H.
vector_string.caption: Vector String

vendor_name.description: The vendor that provided the CVSS score. For example: NVD, REDHAT etc.
vendor_name.caption: Vendor Name

cvss.name: cvss
cvss.description: The Common Vulnerability Scoring System (CVSS) object provides a way to capture the principal characteristics of a vulnerability and produce a numerical score reflecting its severity.
cvss.caption: CVSS Score

policy.description: Details about the Identity/Access management policies that are applicable.
policy.caption: Policy

authorization.name: authorization
authorization.description: The Authorization Result object provides details about the authorization outcome and associated policies related to activity.
authorization.caption: Authorization Result

name.description: Human-readable model name. For example: gpt-4o, claude-3-sonnet, or text-embedding-ada-002.
name.caption: Name

version.description: Model version identifier. For example: 2024-05-13, v2.1.0, or beta.

references.description: AI Model Registry and Documentation Standards

cis_controls.description: The CIS Critical Security Controls is a prioritized set of actions to protect your organization and data from cyber-attack vectors.

cis_benchmark.name: cis_benchmark
cis_benchmark.description: The CIS Benchmark object describes best practices for securely configuring IT systems, software, networks, and cloud infrastructure as defined by the Center for Internet Security. See also Getting to Know the CIS Benchmarks.
cis_benchmark.caption: CIS Benchmark
