3.description: Statistical analytics pertains to analyzing data patterns and anomalies using statistical models to predict, detect, and respond to potential threats, enhancing overall security posture through informed decision-making.
3.caption: Statistical

6.description: Tagging refers to the practice of assigning labels or identifiers to data, users, assets, or activities to monitor, control access, and facilitate incident response across various security domains such as DLP and EDR.
6.caption: Tagging

1.description: A Rule in security analytics refers to predefined criteria or conditions set to monitor, alert, or enforce policies, playing a crucial role in access control, threat detection, and regulatory compliance across security systems.
1.caption: Rule

2.description: Behavioral analytics focus on monitoring and analyzing user or system actions to identify deviations from established patterns, aiding in the detection of insider threats, fraud, and advanced persistent threats (APTs).
2.caption: Behavioral

99.description: The type is not mapped. See the type attribute, which contains a data source specific value.
99.caption: Other

4.description: Learning (ML/DL) encompasses techniques that can "learn" from known data to create analytics that generalize to new data. There may be a statistical component to these techniques, but it is not a requirement.
4.caption: Learning (ML/DL)

5.description: Fingerprinting is the technique of collecting detailed system data, including software versions and configurations, to enhance threat detection, data loss prevention (DLP), and endpoint detection and response (EDR) capabilities.
5.caption: Fingerprinting

7.description: Keyword Match involves scanning content for specific terms to identify sensitive information, potential threats, or policy violations, aiding in DLP and compliance monitoring.
7.caption: Keyword Match

8.description: Regular Expressions are used to define complex search patterns for identifying, validating, and extracting specific data sets or threats within digital content, enhancing DLP, EDR, and threat detection mechanisms.
8.caption: Regular Expressions

9.description: Exact Data Match is a precise comparison technique used to detect the unauthorized use or exposure of specific, sensitive information, crucial for enforcing DLP policies and protecting against data breaches.
9.caption: Exact Data Match

10.description: Partial Data Match involves identifying instances where segments of sensitive information or patterns match, facilitating nuanced DLP and threat detection without requiring complete data conformity.
10.caption: Partial Data Match

11.description: Indexed Data Match refers to comparing content against a pre-compiled index of sensitive information to efficiently detect and prevent unauthorized access or breaches, streamlining DLP and compliance efforts.
11.caption: Indexed Data Match

is_mfa.caption: Multi Factor Authentication

is_remote.description: The indication of whether the session is remote.
is_remote.caption: Remote

is_vpn.description: The indication of whether the session is a VPN session.
is_vpn.caption: VPN Session

uid_alt.description: The alternate unique identifier of the session. e.g. AWS ARN - arn:aws:sts::123344444444:assumed-role/Admin/example-session.
uid_alt.caption: Alternate ID

created_time_dt.description: The time when the session was created.
created_time_dt.caption: Created Time

session.name: session
session.description: The Session object describes details about an authenticated session. e.g. Session Creation Time, Session Issuer.
session.caption: Session

attack.description: The MITRE ATT&CK technique that these privileges could enable.
attack.caption: MITRE ATT&CK® Details

privilege_info_list.description: The list of privilege information objects, where each element describes a specific privilege that could enable the associated attack technique.
privilege_info_list.caption: Privilege Info List

privilege_attack_info.name: privilege_attack_info
privilege_attack_info.description: The Privilege Attack Info object groups privileges by the potential attack they could enable. It maps specific privileges to MITRE ATT&CK techniques, helping identify security risks associated with granted permissions.
privilege_attack_info.caption: Privilege Attack Info

name.description: The name of the attack sub-technique. For example: Scanning IP Blocks or User Execution: Unsafe ML Artifacts.
name.caption: Name

uid.description: The unique identifier of the attack sub-technique. For example: T1595.001 or AML.T0011.000.
uid.caption: Unique ID

src_url.description: The versioned permalink of the attack sub-technique. For example: https://attack.mitre.org/versions/v14/techniques/T1595/001/.
src_url.caption: Source URL

sub_technique.name: sub_technique
sub_technique.description: The MITRE Sub-technique object describes the ATT&CK® or ATLAS™ Sub-technique ID and/or name associated to an attack.
sub_technique.caption: MITRE Sub-technique

value.description: The string value, truncated if is_truncated is true.
value.caption: Value

is_truncated.description: Indicates that value has been truncated. May be omitted if truncation has not occurred.
is_truncated.caption: Is Truncated

untruncated_size.description: The size in bytes of the string represented by value before truncation. Should be omitted if truncation has not occurred.
untruncated_size.caption: Untruncated Size

long_string.name: long_string
long_string.description: This object is a used to capture strings which may be truncated by a security product due to their length.
long_string.caption: Long String

name.description: The name of the aircraft, such as the such as the flight name or callsign.
name.caption: Name

speed.description: Ground speed of flight. This value is provided in meters per second with a minimum resolution of 0.25 m/s. Special Values: Invalid, No Value, or Unknown: 255 m/s.
speed.caption: Speed

location.description: The detailed geographical location usually associated with an IP address.
location.caption: Geo Location

uid.description: The primary identification identifier for an aircraft, such as the 24-bit International Civil Aviation Organization (ICAO) identifier of the aircraft, as 6 hex digits.
uid.caption: Unique ID

model.description: The model name of the aircraft or unmanned system.
model.caption: Model

serial_number.description: The serial number of the aircraft.
serial_number.caption: Serial Number

speed_accuracy.description: Provides quality/containment on horizontal ground speed. Measured in meters/second.
speed_accuracy.caption: Speed Accuracy

track_direction.description: Direction of flight expressed as a “True North-based” ground track angle. This value is provided in clockwise degrees with a minimum resolution of 1 degree. If aircraft is not moving horizontally, use the “Unknown” value
track_direction.caption: Track Direction

uid_alt.description: A secondary identification identifier for an aircraft, such as the 4-digit squawk (octal representation).
uid_alt.caption: Alternate ID

vertical_speed.description: Vertical speed upward relative to the WGS-84 datum, measured in meters per second. Special Values: Invalid, No Value, or Unknown: 63 m/s.
vertical_speed.caption: Vertical Speed

aircraft.name: aircraft
aircraft.description: The Aircraft object represents any aircraft or otherwise airborne asset such as an unmanned system, airplane, balloon, spacecraft, or otherwise. The Aircraft object is intended to normalized data captured or otherwise logged from active radar, passive radar, multi-spectral systems, or the Automatic Dependant Broadcast - Surveillance (ADS-B), and/or Mode S systems.
aircraft.caption: Aircraft

analysis_targets.description: The analysis targets define the scope of monitored activities, specifying what entities, systems or processes are analyzed for activity patterns.
analysis_targets.caption: Analysis Targets

anomalies.description: List of detected activities that significantly deviate from the established baselines. This can include unusual access patterns, unexpected user-agents, abnormal API usage, suspicious traffic spikes, unauthorized access attempts, and other activities that may indicate potential security threats or system issues.
anomalies.caption: Anomalies

baselines.description: List of established patterns representing normal activity that serve as reference points for anomaly detection. This includes typical user interaction patterns like common user-agents, expected API access frequencies and patterns, standard resource utilization levels, and regular traffic flows. These baselines help establish what constitutes 'normal' activity in the system.
baselines.caption: Baselines

anomaly_analysis.name: anomaly_analysis
anomaly_analysis.description: Describes the analysis of activity patterns and anomalies of target entities to identify potential security threats, performance issues, or other deviations from established baselines. This includes monitoring and analyzing user interactions, API usage, resource utilization, access patterns and other measured indicators.
anomaly_analysis.caption: Anomaly Analysis

first_seen_time.description: The timestamp when this identity was first observed or created in the system. This helps establish the identity's age and lifecycle stage for risk assessment.
first_seen_time.caption: First Seen

last_authentication_time.description: The timestamp when this identity last successfully authenticated to any system or service. This differs from last_seen_time as it specifically tracks authentication events rather than all activities.
last_authentication_time.caption: Last Authentication Time

last_seen_time.description: The timestamp of the most recent activity performed by this identity, including authentication, resource access, or API calls. This is the most comprehensive indicator of identity usage recency.
last_seen_time.caption: Last Seen

password_last_used_time.description: The timestamp when password-based authentication was last used by this identity. This helps distinguish between password and other authentication methods (MFA, SSO, certificates) and identify password-specific usage patterns.
password_last_used_time.caption: Password Last Used Time

programmatic_credentials.description: Details about the programmatic credentials associated with this identity, such as API keys, service account keys, access tokens, and client certificates used for automated access.
programmatic_credentials.caption: Programmatic Credentials

first_seen_time_dt.description: The timestamp when this identity was first observed or created in the system. This helps establish the identity's age and lifecycle stage for risk assessment.
first_seen_time_dt.caption: First Seen

last_authentication_time_dt.description: The timestamp when this identity last successfully authenticated to any system or service. This differs from last_seen_time as it specifically tracks authentication events rather than all activities.
last_authentication_time_dt.caption: Last Authentication Time

last_seen_time_dt.description: The timestamp of the most recent activity performed by this identity, including authentication, resource access, or API calls. This is the most comprehensive indicator of identity usage recency.
last_seen_time_dt.caption: Last Seen

password_last_used_time_dt.description: The timestamp when password-based authentication was last used by this identity. This helps distinguish between password and other authentication methods (MFA, SSO, certificates) and identify password-specific usage patterns.
password_last_used_time_dt.caption: Password Last Used Time

identity_activity_metrics.name: identity_activity_metrics
identity_activity_metrics.description: The Identity Activity Metrics object captures usage patterns, authentication activity, credential usage and other metrics for identities across cloud and on-premises environments. Example identities include AWS IAM Users, Roles, Azure AD Principals, GCP Service Accounts, on-premises Active Directory accounts.
identity_activity_metrics.caption: Identity Activity Metrics

state.description: The security state, normalized to the caption of the state_id value. In the case of 'Other', it is defined by the source.
state.caption: Security State

3.description: Isolated from the network.
3.caption: In network quarantine

6.description: The security solution does not have a valid license.
6.caption: Protection not licensed

1.description: The content is missing or outdated.
1.caption: Missing or outdated content

2.description: Not in compliance with the expected security policy.
2.caption: Policy mismatch

99.description: The security state is not mapped. See the state attribute, which contains data source specific values.
99.caption: Other

4.description: Not protected by a security solution.
4.caption: Protection off

5.description: The security solution is not functioning properly.
5.caption: Protection malfunction

7.description: A detected threat has not been remediated.
7.caption: Unremediated threat

8.description: Reputation of the entity is suspicious.
8.caption: Suspicious reputation

9.description: A reboot is required for one or more pending actions.
9.caption: Reboot pending

10.description: The content is locked to a specific version.
10.caption: Content is locked

11.description: The entity is not installed.
11.caption: Not installed

12.description: The system partition is writeable.
12.caption: Writable system partition

14.description: The device has failed the boot verification process.
14.caption: Failed boot verify

15.description: The execution environment has been modified.
15.caption: Modified execution environment

16.description: The SELinux security feature has been disabled.
16.caption: SELinux disabled

17.description: An elevated privilege shell has been detected.
17.caption: Elevated privilege shell

18.description: The file system has been altered on an iOS device.
18.caption: iOS file system altered

20.description: Mobile OTA (Over The Air) updates have been disabled.
20.caption: OTA updates disabled

21.description: The device has been modified to allow root access.
21.caption: Rooted

22.description: The Android partition has been modified.
22.caption: Android partition modified

23.description: The entity is not compliant with the associated security policy.
23.caption: Compliance failure

13.description: The device has failed the SafetyNet check.
13.caption: SafetyNet failure

19.description: Remote access is enabled.
19.caption: Open remote access

state_id.description: The security state of the managed entity.
state_id.caption: Security State ID

security_state.name: security_state
security_state.description: The Security State object describes the security related state of a managed entity.
security_state.caption: Security State

scale_factor.description: The numeric scale factor of display.
scale_factor.caption: Scale Factor

physical_height.description: The numeric physical height of display.
physical_height.caption: Physical Height

physical_orientation.description: The numeric physical orientation of display.
physical_orientation.caption: Physical Orientation

physical_width.description: The numeric physical width of display.
physical_width.caption: Physical Width

display.name: display
display.description: The Display object contains information about the physical or virtual display connected to a computer system.
display.caption: Display

analyzed_privileges_count.description: The total count of privileges that were analyzed across all services.
analyzed_privileges_count.caption: Analyzed Privileges Count

condition_keys.description: The condition keys and their values that were evaluated during policy analysis, including contextual constraints that affect permission grants. These conditions define when and how permissions are applied. Examples: aws:SourceIp:1.2.3.4, aws:RequestedRegion:us-east-1.
condition_keys.caption: Condition Keys

granted_privileges.description: The specific privileges, actions, or permissions that are explicitly granted by the analyzed policy. Examples: AWS actions like s3:GetObject, ec2:RunInstances, iam:CreateUser; Azure actions like Microsoft.Storage/storageAccounts/read; or GCP permissions like storage.objects.get.
granted_privileges.caption: Granted Privileges

policy.description: Detailed information about the policy document that was analyzed, including policy metadata, version, type (identity-based, resource-based, etc.), and structural details. This provides context for understanding the scope and nature of the permission analysis.
policy.caption: Policy

service_privilege_analysis_list.description: The list of privilege analysis results grouped by cloud service or namespace.
service_privilege_analysis_list.caption: Service Privilege Analysis List

total_potential_attacks_count.description: The total count of privilege-to-attack technique mappings identified across all analyzed privileges.
total_potential_attacks_count.caption: Total Potential Attacks Count

unused_privileges_count.description: The total count of privileges or actions defined in the policy that have not been utilized within the analysis timeframe. This metric helps identify over-privileged access and opportunities for privilege reduction to follow the principle of least privilege. High counts may indicate policy bloat or excessive permissions.
unused_privileges_count.caption: Unused Privileges Count

unused_services_count.description: The total count of cloud services or resource types referenced in the policy that have not been accessed or utilized within the analysis timeframe. This helps identify unused service permissions that could be removed to reduce attack surface. Examples: AWS services like S3, SQS, IAM, Lambda; Azure services like Storage, Compute, KeyVault; or GCP services like Cloud Storage, Compute Engine, BigQuery.
unused_services_count.caption: Unused Services Count

permission_analysis_result.name: permission_analysis_result
permission_analysis_result.description: The Permission Analysis object describes analysis results of permissions, policies directly associated with an identity (user, role, or service account). This evaluates what permissions an identity has been granted through attached policies, which privileges are actively used versus unused, and identifies potential over-privileged access. Use this for identity-centric security assessments such as privilege audits, dormant permission discovery, and least-privilege compliance analysis.
permission_analysis_result.caption: Permission Analysis Result

code.description: A numerical response status code providing details about the connection.
code.caption: Response Code

network_endpoint.description: Provides characteristics of the network endpoint.
network_endpoint.caption: Network Endpoint

endpoint_connection.name: endpoint_connection
endpoint_connection.description: The Endpoint Connection object contains information detailing a connection attempt to an endpoint.
endpoint_connection.caption: Endpoint Connection

data.description: The enrichment data associated with the attribute and value. The meaning of this data depends on the type the enrichment record.
data.caption: Data

name.description: The name of the attribute to which the enriched data pertains.
name.caption: Name

type.description: The enrichment type. For example: location.
type.caption: Type

value.description: The value of the attribute to which the enriched data pertains.
value.caption: Value

desc.description: A long description of the enrichment data.
desc.caption: Description

created_time.description: The time when the enrichment data was generated.
created_time.caption: Created Time

reputation.description: The reputation of the enrichment data.
reputation.caption: Reputation Scores

short_desc.description: A short description of the enrichment data.
short_desc.caption: Short Description

src_url.description: The URL of the source of the enrichment data.
src_url.caption: Source URL

created_time_dt.description: The time when the enrichment data was generated.
created_time_dt.caption: Created Time

enrichment.name: enrichment
enrichment.description: The Enrichment object provides inline enrichment data for specific attributes of interest within an event. It serves as a mechanism to enhance or supplement the information associated with the event by adding additional relevant details or context.
enrichment.caption: Enrichment

message.description: The message in a span (often referred to as a span event) serves as a way to record significant moments or occurrences during the span's lifecycle. This content typically manifests as log entries, annotations, or semi-structured events as a string, providing additional granularity and context about what happens at specific points during the execution of an operation.
message.caption: Message

service.description: Identifies the service or component that generates the span, helping trace its path through the distributed system.
service.caption: Service

uid.description: The unique identifier for the span, used in distributed systems and microservices architectures to track and correlate requests across different components of an application. It enables tracing the flow of a request through various services.
uid.caption: Unique ID

operation.description: Describes an action performed in a span, such as API requests, database queries, or computations.
operation.caption: Operation

start_time.description: The start timestamp of the span, essential for identifying latency and performance bottlenecks. This timestamp is normalized across the observability system, ensuring consistency even when events occur across distributed services with potentially unsynchronized clocks. By using normalized time, observability tools can provide accurate, uniform measurements of operation performance and latency, regardless of where or when the events actually occur.
start_time.caption: Start Time

duration.description: The total time, in milliseconds, that the span represents, calculated as the difference between start_time and end_time. It reflects the operation's performance and latency, independent of event timestamps, and accounts for normalized times used by observability tools to ensure consistency across distributed systems.
duration.caption: Duration Milliseconds

end_time.description: The end timestamp of the span, essential for identifying latency and performance bottlenecks. Like the start time, this timestamp is normalized across the observability system to ensure consistency, even when events are recorded across distributed services with unsynchronized clocks. Normalized time allows for accurate duration calculations and helps observability tools track performance across services, regardless of the individual system time settings.
end_time.caption: End Time

start_time_dt.description: The start timestamp of the span, essential for identifying latency and performance bottlenecks. This timestamp is normalized across the observability system, ensuring consistency even when events occur across distributed services with potentially unsynchronized clocks. By using normalized time, observability tools can provide accurate, uniform measurements of operation performance and latency, regardless of where or when the events actually occur.
start_time_dt.caption: Start Time

end_time_dt.description: The end timestamp of the span, essential for identifying latency and performance bottlenecks. Like the start time, this timestamp is normalized across the observability system to ensure consistency, even when events are recorded across distributed services with unsynchronized clocks. Normalized time allows for accurate duration calculations and helps observability tools track performance across services, regardless of the individual system time settings.
end_time_dt.caption: End Time

parent_uid.description: The ID of the parent span for this span object, establishing its relationship in the trace hierarchy.
parent_uid.caption: Parent Unique ID

status_code.description: Indicates the outcome of the operation in the span, such as success, failure, or error. Issues in a span typically refer to problems such as failed operations, timeouts, service unavailability, or errors in processing that can negatively impact the performance or reliability of the system. Tracking the `status_code` helps pinpoint these issues, enabling quicker identification and resolution of system inefficiencies or faults.
status_code.caption: Status Code

span.name: span
span.description: Represents a single unit of work or operation within a distributed trace. A span typically tracks the execution of a request across a service, capturing important details such as the operation, timestamps, and status. Spans help break down the overall trace into smaller, manageable parts, enabling detailed analysis of the performance and behavior of specific operations within the system. They are crucial for understanding latency, dependencies, and bottlenecks in complex distributed systems.
span.caption: Span

version.description: The certificate version.
version.caption: Version

uid.description: The unique identifier of the certificate.
uid.caption: Unique ID

is_self_signed.description: Denotes whether a digital certificate is self-signed or signed by a known certificate authority (CA).
is_self_signed.caption: Certificate Self-Signed

fingerprints.description: The fingerprint list of the certificate.
fingerprints.caption: Fingerprints

created_time.description: The time when the certificate was created.
created_time.caption: Created Time

expiration_time.description: The expiration time of the certificate.
expiration_time.caption: Expiration Time

sans.description: The list of subject alternative names that are secured by a specific certificate.
sans.caption: Subject Alternative Names

serial_number.description: The serial number of the certificate used to create the digital signature.
serial_number.caption: Certificate Serial Number

created_time_dt.description: The time when the certificate was created.
created_time_dt.caption: Created Time

expiration_time_dt.description: The expiration time of the certificate.
expiration_time_dt.caption: Expiration Time

certificate.name: certificate
certificate.description: The Digital Certificate, also known as a Public Key Certificate, object contains information about the ownership and usage of a public key. It serves as a means to establish trust in the authenticity and integrity of the public key and the associated entity.
certificate.caption: Digital Certificate

name.description: The name of the key.
name.caption: Name

value.description: The value associated to the key.
value.caption: Value

values.description: Optional, the values associated to the key. You can populate this attribute, when you have multiple values for the same key.
values.caption: Values

key_value_object.name: key_value_object
key_value_object.description: A generic object allowing to define a {key:value} pair.
key_value_object.caption: Key:Value object

desc.description: The description of the geographical location.
desc.caption: Description

long.description: The geographical Longitude coordinate represented in Decimal Degrees (DD). For example: -71.057083.
long.caption: Longitude

country.description:
The ISO 3166-1 Alpha-2 country code.

Note: The two letter country code should be capitalized. For example: US or CA.
country.caption: Country

coordinates.description: A two-element array, containing a longitude/latitude pair. The format conforms with GeoJSON. For example: [-73.983, 40.719].
coordinates.caption: Coordinates

aerial_height.description: Expressed as either height above takeoff location or height above ground level (AGL) for a UAS current location. This value is provided in meters and must have a minimum resolution of 1 m. Special Values: Invalid, No Value, or Unknown: -1000 m.
aerial_height.caption: Aerial Height

geodetic_altitude.description: The aircraft distance above or below the ellipsoid as measured along a line that passes through the aircraft and is normal to the surface of the WGS-84 ellipsoid. This value is provided in meters and must have a minimum resolution of 1 m. Special Values: Invalid, No Value, or Unknown: -1000 m.
geodetic_altitude.caption: Geodetic Altitude

geodetic_vertical_accuracy.description: Provides quality/containment on geodetic altitude. This is based on ADS-B Geodetic Vertical Accuracy (GVA). Measured in meters.
geodetic_vertical_accuracy.caption: Geodetic Vertical Accuracy

geohash.description:
Geohash of the geo-coordinates (latitude and longitude).

Geohashing is a geocoding system used to encode geographic coordinates in decimal degrees, to a single string.
geohash.caption: Geohash

horizontal_accuracy.description: Provides quality/containment on horizontal position. This is based on ADS-B NACp. Measured in meters.
horizontal_accuracy.caption: Horizontal Accuracy

is_on_premises.description: Indicates whether the location is on-premises.
is_on_premises.caption: On-Premises

isp.description: The name of the Internet Service Provider (ISP).
isp.caption: ISP Name

@deprecated.message: Utilize isp attribute available in network_endpoint, whois objects according to your use-case.

lat.description: The geographical Latitude coordinate represented in Decimal Degrees (DD). For example: 42.361145.
lat.caption: Latitude

postal_code.description: The postal code of the location.
postal_code.caption: Postal Code

pressure_altitude.description: The uncorrected barometric pressure altitude (based on reference standard 29.92 inHg, 1013.25 mb) provides a reference for algorithms that utilize 'altitude deltas' between aircraft. This value is provided in meters and must have a minimum resolution of 1 m.. Special Values: Invalid, No Value, or Unknown: -1000 m.
pressure_altitude.caption: Pressure Altitude

provider.description: The provider of the geographical location data.
provider.caption: Provider

region.description: The alphanumeric code that identifies the principal subdivision (e.g. province or state) of the country. For example, 'CH-VD' for the Canton of Vaud, Switzerland
region.caption: Region

location.name: location
location.description: The Geo Location object describes a geographical location, usually associated with an IP address.
location.caption: Geo Location

module.description: The module that pertains to the event when query_type_id indicates a Module query.
module.caption: Module

process.description: The process that pertains to the event when query_type_id indicates a Process query.

session.description: The authenticated user or service session when query_type_id indicates a Session query.

file.description: The file that is the target of the query when query_type_id indicates a File query.

state.description: The state of the socket, normalized to the caption of the state_id value. In the case of 'Other', it is defined by the event source.

user.description: The user that pertains to the event when query_type_id indicates a User query.
user.caption: User

kernel.description: The kernel object that pertains to the event when query_type_id indicates a Kernel query.

service.description: The service that pertains to the event when query_type_id indicates a Service query.

group.description: The administrative group that is the target of the query when query_type_id indicates an Admin Group query.

users.description: The users that belong to the administrative group when query_type_id indicates a Users query.
users.caption: Users

connection_info.description: The network connection information related to a Network Connection query type.
connection_info.caption: Connection Info

folder.description: The folder that is the target of the query when query_type_id indicates a Folder query.
folder.caption: Folder

job.description: The job object that pertains to the event when query_type_id indicates a Job query.
job.caption: Job

network_interfaces.description: The physical or virtual network interfaces that are associated with the device when query_type_id indicates a Network Interfaces query.
network_interfaces.caption: Network Interfaces

peripheral_device.description: The peripheral device that triggered the event when query_type_id indicates a Peripheral Device query.
peripheral_device.caption: Peripheral Device

query_type.description: The normalized caption of query_type_id or the source-specific query type.
query_type.caption: Query Type

3.description: A query about folder attributes, metadata, content, or structure.
3.caption: Folder

6.description: A query about loaded modules, their base addresses, load types, or function entry points.
6.caption: Module

0.description: The query type was unknown or not specified.
0.caption: Unknown

1.description: A query about kernel resources including system calls, shared mutex, or other kernel components.
1.caption: Kernel

2.description: A query about file attributes, metadata, content, hash values, or properties.
2.caption: File

99.description: The query type was not mapped to a standard category. See the query_type attribute for source-specific value.
99.caption: Other

4.description: A query about group membership, privileges, domain, or group properties.
4.caption: Admin Group

5.description: A query about scheduled jobs, their command lines, run states, or execution times.
5.caption: Job

7.description: A query about active network connections, boundaries, protocols, or TCP states.
7.caption: Network Connection

8.description: A query about physical or virtual network interfaces, their IP/MAC addresses, or types.
8.caption: Network Interfaces

9.description: A query about attached peripheral devices, their classes, models, or vendor information.
9.caption: Peripheral Device

10.description: A query about running processes, command lines, ancestry, loaded modules, or execution context.
10.caption: Process

11.description: A query about system services, their names, versions, labels, or properties.
11.caption: Service

12.description: A query about authenticated user or service sessions, their creation times, or issuer details.
12.caption: Session

14.description: A query about multiple users belonging to an administrative group.
14.caption: Users

15.description: A query about startup configuration items, their run modes, start types, or current states.
15.caption: Startup Item

16.description: A Windows-specific query about registry keys, their paths, security descriptors, or modification times.
16.caption: Registry Key

17.description: A Windows-specific query about registry values, their data types, content, or names.
17.caption: Registry Value

18.description: A Windows-specific query about prefetch files, their run counts, last execution times, or existence.
18.caption: Prefetch

13.description: A query about user accounts, their properties, credentials, or domain information.
13.caption: User

query_type_id.description: The normalized type of system query performed against a device or system component.
query_type_id.caption: Query Type ID

startup_item.description: The startup item object that pertains to the event when query_type_id indicates a Startup Item query.
startup_item.caption: Startup Item

3.description: The socket has passively received a connection request from a remote peer.
3.caption: SYN-RECEIVED

6.description: The socket connection has been closed by the local application, the remote peer has closed its half of the connection, and the system is waiting to be sure that the remote peer received the last acknowledgement.
6.caption: TIME-WAIT

1.description: The socket has an established connection between a local application and a remote peer.
1.caption: ESTABLISHED

2.description: The socket is actively trying to establish a connection to a remote peer.
2.caption: SYN-SENT

4.description: The socket connection has been closed by the local application, the remote peer has not yet acknowledged the close, and the system is waiting for it to close its half of the connection.
4.caption: FIN-WAIT-1

5.description: The socket connection has been closed by the local application, the remote peer has acknowledged the close, and the system is waiting for it to close its half of the connection.
5.caption: FIN-WAIT-2

7.description: The socket is not in use.
7.caption: CLOSED

8.description: The socket connection has been closed by the remote peer, and the system is waiting for the local application to close its half of the connection.
8.caption: CLOSE-WAIT

9.description: The socket connection has been closed by the remote peer, the local application has closed its half of the connection, and the system is waiting for the remote peer to acknowledge the close.
9.caption: LAST-ACK

10.description: The socket is listening for incoming connections.
10.caption: LISTEN

11.description: The socket connection has been closed by the local application and the remote peer simultaneously, and the remote peer has not yet acknowledged the close attempt of the local application.
11.caption: CLOSING

tcp_state_id.description: The state of the TCP socket for the network connection.
tcp_state_id.caption: TCP State ID

reg_key.description: The registry key object describes a Windows registry key.
reg_key.caption: Registry Key

reg_value.description: The registry key object describes a Windows registry value.
reg_value.caption: Registry Value

query_evidence.name: query_evidence
query_evidence.description: The specific resulting evidence information that was queried or discovered. When mapping raw telemetry data users should select the appropriate child object that best matches the evidence type as defined by query_type_id.
query_evidence.caption: Query Evidence

count.description: The number of discovered entities of the specified type.
count.caption: Count

type.description: The specific type of information that was discovered. e.g. name, phone_number, etc.
type.caption: Type

value.description: Optionally, the specific value of discovered information.
value.caption: Value

occurrence_details.description: Details about where in the target entity, specified information was discovered. Only the attributes, relevant to the target entity type should be populated.
occurrence_details.caption: Occurrence Details

occurrences.description: Details about where in the target entity, specified information was discovered. Only the attributes, relevant to the target entity type should be populated.
occurrences.caption: Occurrences

discovery_details.name: discovery_details
discovery_details.description: The Discovery Details object describes results of a discovery task/job.
discovery_details.caption: Discovery Details

modified_time.description: The time when the file was last modified.
modified_time.caption: Modified Time

ext.description: The extension of the file, excluding the leading dot. For example: exe from svchost.exe, or gz from export.tar.gz.
ext.caption: File Extension

uid.description: The unique identifier of the file as defined by the storage system, such the file system file ID.
uid.caption: Unique ID

parent_folder.description: The parent folder in which the file resides. For example: c:\windows\system32
parent_folder.caption: Parent Folder

is_public.description: Indicates if the file is publicly accessible. For example in an object's public access in AWS S3
is_public.caption: Public

is_deleted.description: Indicates if the file was deleted from the filesystem.
is_deleted.caption: Deleted

mime_type.description: The Multipurpose Internet Mail Extensions (MIME) type of the file, if applicable.
mime_type.caption: MIME type

owner.description: The user that owns the file/object.
owner.caption: Owner

is_system.description: The indication of whether the object is part of the operating system.
is_system.caption: System

type.description: The file type.
type.caption: Type

signature.description: The digital signature of the file.
signature.caption: Digital Signature

attributes.description: The bitmask value that represents the file attributes.
attributes.caption: Attributes

uri.description: The file URI, such as those reporting by static analysis tools. E.g., file:///C:/dev/sarif/sarif-tutorials/samples/Introduction/simple-example.js
uri.caption: File URI

99.description: The type is not mapped. See the type attribute, which contains a data source specific value.
99.caption: Other

type_id.description: The file type ID. Note the distinction between a Regular File and an Executable File. If the distinction is not known, or not indicated by the log, use Regular File. In this case, it should not be assumed that a Regular File is not executable.
type_id.caption: Type ID

xattributes.description:
An unordered collection of zero or more name/value pairs where each pair represents a file or folder extended attribute.

For example: Windows alternate data stream attributes (ADS stream name, ADS size, etc.), user-defined or application-defined attributes, ACL, owner, primary group, etc. Examples from DCS:

  • ads_name
  • ads_size
  • dacl
  • owner
  • primary_group
  • link_name - name of the link associated to the file.
  • hard_link_count - the number of links that are associated to the file.
xattributes.caption: Extended Attributes

modified_time_dt.description: The time when the file was last modified.
modified_time_dt.caption: Modified Time

imported_symbols.description: A list of symbols imported by the executable file.
imported_symbols.caption: Imported Symbols

product.description: The product that created or installed the file.
product.caption: Product

is_encrypted.description: Indicates if the file is encrypted.
is_encrypted.caption: Encrypted

internal_name.description: The name of the file as identified within the file itself. This contrasts with the name by which the file is known on disk. Where available, the internal name is widely used by security practitioners and detection content because the on-disk file name is not reliable. On the Windows OS, most PE files contain a VERSIONINFO resource from which the internal name can be obtained. On macOS, binaries can optionally embed a copy of the application's Info.plist file which in turn contains the name of the executable.
internal_name.caption: Internal Name

name.description: The name of the file. For example: svchost.exe
name.caption: Name

url.description: The URL of the file, when applicable.
url.caption: URL

accessed_time.description: The time when the file was last accessed.
accessed_time.caption: Accessed Time

desc.description: The description of the file, as returned by file system. For example: the description as returned by the Unix file command or the Windows file type.
desc.caption: Description

created_time.description: The time when the file was created.
created_time.caption: Created Time

99.description: The confidentiality is not mapped. See the confidentiality attribute, which contains a data source specific value.
99.caption: Other

confidentiality_id.description: The normalized identifier of the file content confidentiality indicator.
confidentiality_id.caption: Confidentiality ID

data_classification.description: The Data Classification object includes information about data classification levels and data category types.
data_classification.caption: Data Classification

created_time_dt.description: The time when the file was created.
created_time_dt.caption: Created Time

data_classifications.description: A list of Data Classification objects, that include information about data classification levels and data category types, identified by a classifier.
data_classifications.caption: Data Classification

version.description: The file version. For example: 8.0.7601.17514.
version.caption: Version

tags.description: The list of tags; {key:value} pairs associated to the file.
tags.caption: Tags

accessed_time_dt.description: The time when the file was last accessed.
accessed_time_dt.caption: Accessed Time

accessor.description: The name of the user who last accessed the object.
accessor.caption: Accessor

volume.description: The volume on the storage device where the file is located.
volume.caption: Volume

path.description: The full path to the file. For example: c:\windows\system32\svchost.exe.
path.caption: Path

creator.description: The user that created the file.
creator.caption: Creator

modifier.description: The user that last modified the file.
modifier.caption: Modifier

3.description: The drive is a remote (network) drive.
3.caption: Remote

1.description: The drive has removable media; for example, a floppy drive, thumb drive, or flash card reader.
1.caption: Removable

2.description: The drive has fixed media; for example, a hard disk drive or flash drive.
2.caption: Fixed

99.description: The drive type is not mapped. See the drive_type attribute, which contains a data source specific value.
99.caption: Other

4.description: The drive is a CD-ROM drive.
4.caption: CD-ROM

5.description: The drive is a RAM disk.
5.caption: RAM Disk

drive_type_id.description: Identifies the type of a disk drive, i.e. fixed, removable, etc.
drive_type_id.caption: Drive Type ID

is_readonly.description: Indicates that the file cannot be modified.
is_readonly.caption: Read-Only

size.description: The size of data, in bytes.
size.caption: Size

storage_class.description: The storage class of the file. For example in AWS S3: STANDARD, STANDARD_IA, GLACIER.
storage_class.caption: Storage Class

confidentiality.description: The file content confidentiality, normalized to the confidentiality_id value. In the case of 'Other', it is defined by the event source.
confidentiality.caption: Confidentiality

encryption_details.description: The encryption details of the file. Should be populated if the file is encrypted.
encryption_details.caption: Encryption Details

drive_type.description: The drive type, normalized to the caption of the drive_type_id value. In the case of Other, it is defined by the source.
drive_type.caption: Drive Type

company_name.description: The name of the company that published the file. For example: Microsoft Corporation.
company_name.caption: Company Name

file.name: file
file.description: The File object represents the metadata associated with a file stored in a computer system. It encompasses information about the file itself, including its attributes, properties, and organizational details.
file.caption: File

name.description: The name of the logging product instance.
name.caption: Name

version.description: The version of the logging provider.
version.caption: Version

device.description: The device where the events are logged.
device.caption: Device

product.description: The product logging the event.  This may be the event source product, a management server product, a scanning product, a SIEM, etc.
product.caption: Product

uid.description: The unique identifier of the logging product instance.
uid.caption: Unique ID

log_level.description: The level at which an event was logged. This can be log provider specific. For example the audit level.
log_level.caption: Log Level

event_uid.description: The unique identifier of the event assigned by the logger.
event_uid.caption: Event UID

is_truncated.description: Indicates whether the OCSF event data has been truncated due to size limitations. When true, some event data may have been omitted to fit within system constraints.
is_truncated.caption: Is Truncated

log_format.description: The format of data in the log. For example JSON, syslog or CSV.
log_format.caption: Log Format

log_name.description: The log name for the logging provider log, or the file name of the system log. This may be an intermediate store-and-forward log or a vendor destination log. For example /archive/server1/var/log/messages.0 or /var/log/.
log_name.caption: Log Name

log_provider.description: The logging provider or logging service that logged the event. This may be an intermediate application store-and-forward log or a vendor destination log.
log_provider.caption: Log Provider

log_version.description: The event log schema version of the original event. For example the syslog version or the Cisco Log Schema version
log_version.caption: Log Version

logged_time.description:
The time when the logging system collected and logged the event.

This attribute is distinct from the event time in that event time typically contain the time extracted from the original event. Most of the time, these two times will be different.
logged_time.caption: Logged Time

transmit_time.description: The time when the event was transmitted from the logging device to it's next destination.
transmit_time.caption: Transmission Time

untruncated_size.description: The original size of the OCSF event data in kilobytes before any truncation occurred. This field is typically populated when is_truncated is true to indicate the full size of the original event.
untruncated_size.caption: Untruncated Size

logged_time_dt.description:
The time when the logging system collected and logged the event.

This attribute is distinct from the event time in that event time typically contain the time extracted from the original event. Most of the time, these two times will be different.
logged_time_dt.caption: Logged Time

transmit_time_dt.description: The time when the event was transmitted from the logging device to it's next destination.
transmit_time_dt.caption: Transmission Time

logger.name: logger
logger.description: The Logger object represents the device and product where events are stored with times for receipt and transmission.  This may be at the source device where the event occurred, a remote scanning device, intermediate hops, or the ultimate destination.
logger.caption: Logger

proxy_endpoint.description: The network proxy information pertaining to a specific endpoint. This can be used to describe information pertaining to network address translation (NAT).
proxy_endpoint.caption: Proxy Endpoint

zone.description: The network zone or LAN segment.
zone.caption: Network Zone

uid.description: The unique identifier of the endpoint.
uid.caption: Unique ID

vpc_uid.description: The unique identifier of the Virtual Private Cloud (VPC).
vpc_uid.caption: VPC UID

port.description: The port used for communication within the network connection.
port.caption: Port

0.description: Unknown whether this endpoint resides within the customer’s network.
0.caption: Unknown

1.description: The endpoint resides inside the customer’s network.
1.caption: Internal

2.description: The endpoint is on the Internet or otherwise external to the customer’s network.
2.caption: External

99.description: The network scope is not mapped. See the network_scope attribute, which contains a data source specific value.
99.caption: Other

network_scope_id.description: The normalized identifier of the endpoint’s network scope. The normalized network scope identifier indicates whether the endpoint resides inside the customer’s network, outside on the Internet, or if its location relative to the customer’s network cannot be determined.
network_scope_id.caption: Network Scope ID

fingerprints.description: Fingerprints that identify the specific application implementation on this endpoint, such as Cisco NPF or HASSH.
fingerprints.caption: Fingerprints

owner.description: The identity of the service or user account that owns the endpoint or was last logged into it.
owner.caption: Owner

type.description: The network endpoint type. For example: unknown, server, desktop, laptop, tablet, mobile, virtual, browser, or other.
type.caption: Type

network_scope.description: Indicates whether the endpoint resides inside the customer’s network, outside on the Internet, or if its location relative to the customer’s network cannot be determined. The value is normalized to the caption of the network_scope_id.
network_scope.caption: Network Scope

container.description: The information describing an instance of a container. A container is a prepackaged, portable system image that runs isolated on an existing system using a container runtime like containerd.
container.caption: Container

3.description: A laptop computer.
3.caption: Laptop

6.description: A virtual machine.
6.caption: Virtual

1.description: A server.
1.caption: Server

2.description: A desktop computer.
2.caption: Desktop

99.description: The type is not mapped. See the type attribute, which contains a data source specific value.
99.caption: Other

4.description: A tablet computer.
4.caption: Tablet

5.description: A mobile phone.
5.caption: Mobile

7.description: An IOT (Internet of Things) device.
7.caption: IOT

8.description: A web browser.
8.caption: Browser

9.description: A networking firewall.
9.caption: Firewall

10.description: A networking switch.
10.caption: Switch

11.description: A networking hub.
11.caption: Hub

12.description: A networking router.
12.caption: Router

14.description: An intrusion prevention system.
14.caption: IPS

15.description: A Load Balancer device.
15.caption: Load Balancer

13.description: An intrusion detection system.
13.caption: IDS

type_id.description: The network endpoint type ID.
type_id.caption: Type ID

domain.description: The name of the domain that the endpoint belongs to or that corresponds to the endpoint.
domain.caption: Domain

pool.description: The pool of desktops or virtual machines to which the endpoint belongs.
pool.caption: Pool

interface_name.description: The name of the network interface (e.g. eth2).
interface_name.caption: Network Interface Name

isp.description: The name of the Internet Service Provider (ISP).
isp.caption: ISP Name

namespace_pid.description: If running under a process namespace (such as in a container), the process identifier within that process namespace.
namespace_pid.caption: Namespace PID

mac_vendor.description: The vendor or manufacturer of the endpoint's network interface controller (NIC), as identified from the MAC address.
mac_vendor.caption: MAC Vendor

name.description: The short name of the endpoint.
name.caption: Name

mac.description: The Media Access Control (MAC) address of the endpoint.
mac.caption: MAC Address

instance_uid.description: The unique identifier of a VM instance.
instance_uid.caption: Instance ID

subnet_uid.description: The unique identifier of a virtual subnet.
subnet_uid.caption: Subnet UID

svc_name.description: The service name in service-to-service connections. For example, AWS VPC logs the pkt-src-aws-service and pkt-dst-aws-service fields identify the connection is coming from or going to an AWS service.
svc_name.caption: Service Name

hostname.description: The fully qualified name of the endpoint.
hostname.caption: Hostname

autonomous_system.description: The Autonomous System details associated with an IP address.
autonomous_system.caption: Autonomous System

agent_list.description: A list of agent objects associated with a device, endpoint, or resource.
agent_list.caption: Agent List

interface_uid.description: The unique identifier of the network interface.
interface_uid.caption: Network Interface ID

ip.description: The IP address of the endpoint, in either IPv4 or IPv6 format.
ip.caption: IP Address

isp_org.description: The organization name of the Internet Service Provider (ISP). This represents the parent organization or company that owns/operates the ISP. For example, Comcast Corporation would be the ISP org for Xfinity internet service. This attribute helps identify the ultimate provider when ISPs operate under different brand names.
isp_org.caption: ISP Org

location.description: The geographical location of the endpoint.
location.caption: Geo Location

intermediate_ips.description: The intermediate IP Addresses. For example, the IP addresses in the HTTP X-Forwarded-For header.
intermediate_ips.caption: Intermediate IP Addresses

network_endpoint.name: network_endpoint
network_endpoint.description: The Network Endpoint object describes characteristics of a network endpoint. These can be a source or destination of a network connection.
network_endpoint.caption: Network Endpoint

application.description: Application Activity events report detailed information about the behavior of applications and services.
application.caption: Application Activity

discovery.description: Discovery events report the existence and state of devices, files, configurations, processes, registry keys, and other objects.
discovery.caption: Discovery

findings.description: Findings events report findings, detections, and possible resolutions of malware, anomalies, or other actions performed by security products.
findings.caption: Findings

iam.description: Identity & Access Management (IAM) events relate to the supervision of the system's authentication and access control model. Examples of such events are the success or failure of authentication, granting of authority, password change, entity change, privileged use etc.
iam.caption: Identity & Access Management

remediation.description: Remediation events report the results of remediation commands targeting files, processes, and other objects.
remediation.caption: Remediation

unmanned_systems.description: Unmanned Systems events report the activity, existence, and/or state of unmanned systems for tracking, mission planning, and other related activities.
unmanned_systems.caption: Unmanned Systems

categories.name: category
categories.description: The OCSF categories organize event classes, each aligned with a specific domain or area of focus.
categories.caption: Categories

app.description: The application that was affected by the lifecycle event.  This also applies to self-updating application systems.
app.caption: Application

time.description: The normalized event occurrence time or the finding creation time.
time.caption: Event Time

firewall_rule.description: The firewall rule that pertains to the control that triggered the event, if applicable.
firewall_rule.caption: Firewall Rule

malware.description: A list of Malware objects, describing details about the identified malware.
malware.caption: Malware

type_name.description: The event/finding type name, as defined by the type_uid.
type_name.caption: Type Name

6002.description: Application Lifecycle events report installation, removal, start, stop of an application or service.
6002.caption: Application Lifecycle

class_uid.description: The unique identifier of a class. A class describes the attributes available in an event.
class_uid.caption: Class ID

cloud.description: Describes details about the Cloud environment where the event or finding was created.
cloud.caption: Cloud

3.description: Start the application.
3.caption: Start

6.description: Enable the application.
6.caption: Enable

1.description: Install the application.
1.caption: Install

2.description: Remove the application.
2.caption: Remove

99.description: The event activity is not mapped. See the activity_name attribute, which contains a data source specific value.
99.caption: Other

4.description: Stop the application.
4.caption: Stop

5.description: Restart the application.
5.caption: Restart

7.description: Disable the application.
7.caption: Disable

8.description: Update the application.
8.caption: Update

activity_id.description: The normalized identifier of the activity that triggered the event.
activity_id.caption: Activity ID

raw_data_hash.description: The hash, which describes the content of the raw_data field.
raw_data_hash.caption: Raw Data Hash

risk_details.description: Describes the risk associated with the finding.
risk_details.caption: Risk Details

malware_scan_info.description: Describes details about the scan job that identified malware on the target system.
malware_scan_info.caption: Malware Scan Info

timezone_offset.description: The number of minutes that the reported event time is ahead or behind UTC, in the range -1,080 to +1,080.
timezone_offset.caption: Timezone Offset

3.description: A suspicious file or other content was moved to a benign location.
3.caption: Quarantined

6.description: The request was detected as a threat and resulted in the connection being dropped.
6.caption: Dropped

1.description: Granted access or allowed the action to the protected resource.
1.caption: Allowed

2.description: Denied access or blocked the action to the protected resource.
2.caption: Blocked

99.description: The disposition is not mapped. See the disposition attribute, which contains a data source specific value.
99.caption: Other

4.description: A session was isolated on the network or within a browser.
4.caption: Isolated

5.description: A file or other content was deleted.
5.caption: Deleted

7.description: A custom action was executed such as running of a command script. Use the message attribute of the base class for details.
7.caption: Custom Action

8.description: A request or submission was approved. For example, when a form was properly filled out and submitted. This is distinct from 1 'Allowed'.
8.caption: Approved

9.description: A quarantined file or other content was restored to its original location.
9.caption: Restored

10.description: A suspicious or risky entity was deemed to no longer be suspicious (re-scored).
10.caption: Exonerated

11.description: A corrupt file or configuration was corrected.
11.caption: Corrected

12.description: A corrupt file or configuration was partially corrected.
12.caption: Partially Corrected

14.description: An operation was delayed, for example if a restart was required to finish the operation.
14.caption: Delayed

15.description: Suspicious activity or a policy violation was detected without further action.
15.caption: Detected

16.description: The outcome of an operation had no action taken.
16.caption: No Action

17.description: The operation or action was logged without further action.
17.caption: Logged

18.description: A file or other entity was marked with extended attributes.
18.caption: Tagged

20.description: Counted the request or activity but did not determine whether to allow it or block it.
20.caption: Count

21.description: The request was detected as a threat and resulted in the connection being reset.
21.caption: Reset

22.description: Required the end user to solve a CAPTCHA puzzle to prove that a human being is sending the request.
22.caption: Captcha

23.description: Ran a silent challenge that required the client session to verify that it's a browser, and not a bot.
23.caption: Challenge

24.description: The requestor's access has been revoked due to security policy enforcements. Note: use the Host profile if the User or Actor requestor is not present in the event class.
24.caption: Access Revoked

25.description: A request or submission was rejected. For example, when a form was improperly filled out and submitted. This is distinct from 2 'Blocked'.
25.caption: Rejected

26.description: An attempt to access a resource was denied due to an authorization check that failed. This is a more specific disposition than 2 'Blocked' and can be complemented with the authorizations attribute for more detail.
26.caption: Unauthorized

27.description: An error occurred during the processing of the activity or request. Use the message attribute of the base class for details.
27.caption: Error

13.description: A corrupt file or configuration was not corrected.
13.caption: Uncorrected

19.description: The request or activity was detected as a threat and resulted in a notification but request was not blocked.
19.caption: Alert

disposition_id.description: Describes the outcome or action taken by a security control, such as access control checks, malware detections or various types of policy violations.
disposition_id.caption: Disposition ID

6.description: Application Activity events report detailed information about the behavior of applications and services.
6.caption: Application Activity

category_uid.description: The category unique identifier of the event.
category_uid.caption: Category ID

metadata.description: The metadata associated with the event or a finding.
metadata.caption: Metadata

3.description: The activity was observed, but neither explicitly allowed nor denied. This is common with IDS and EDR controls that report additional information on observed behavior such as TTPs. The disposition_id attribute should be set to a value that conforms to this action, for example 'Logged', 'Alert', 'Detected', 'Count', etc.
3.caption: Observed

0.description: The action was unknown. The disposition_id attribute may still be set to a non-unknown value, for example 'Custom Action', 'Challenge'.
0.caption: Unknown

1.description: The activity was allowed. The disposition_id attribute should be set to a value that conforms to this action, for example 'Allowed', 'Approved', 'Delayed', 'No Action', 'Count' etc.
1.caption: Allowed

2.description: The attempted activity was denied. The disposition_id attribute should be set to a value that conforms to this action, for example 'Blocked', 'Rejected', 'Quarantined', 'Isolated', 'Dropped', 'Access Revoked, etc.
2.caption: Denied

99.description: The action is not mapped. See the action attribute which contains a data source specific value.
99.caption: Other

4.description: The activity was modified, adjusted, or corrected. The disposition_id attribute should be set appropriately, for example 'Restored', 'Corrected', 'Delayed', 'Captcha', 'Tagged'.
4.caption: Modified

action_id.description: The action taken by a control or other policy-based system leading to an outcome or disposition. An unknown action may still correspond to a known disposition. Refer to disposition_id for the outcome of the action.
action_id.caption: Action ID

99.description: The confidence is not mapped to the defined enum values. See the confidence attribute, which contains a data source specific value.
99.caption: Other

confidence_id.description: The normalized confidence refers to the accuracy of the rule that created the finding. A rule with a low confidence means that the finding scope is wide and may create finding reports that may not be malicious in nature.
confidence_id.caption: Confidence ID

start_time.description: The start time of a time period, or the time of the least recent event included in the aggregate event.
start_time.caption: Start Time

attacks.description: An array of MITRE ATT&CK® objects describing identified tactics, techniques & sub-techniques. The objects are compatible with MITRE ATLAS™ tactics, techniques & sub-techniques.
attacks.caption: MITRE ATT&CK® and ATLAS™ Details

risk_level.description: The risk level, normalized to the caption of the risk_level_id value.
risk_level.caption: Risk Level

device.description: An addressable device, computer system or host.
device.caption: Device

osint.description: The OSINT (Open Source Intelligence) object contains details related to an indicator such as the indicator itself, related indicators, geolocation, registrar information, subdomains, analyst commentary, and other contextual information. This information can be used to further enrich a detection or finding by providing decisioning support to other analysts and engineers.
osint.caption: OSINT

actor.description: The actor object describes details about the user/role/process that was the source of the activity. Note that this is not the threat actor of a campaign but may be part of a campaign.
actor.caption: Actor

disposition.description: The disposition name, normalized to the caption of the disposition_id value. In the case of 'Other', it is defined by the event source.
disposition.caption: Disposition

type_uid.description: The event/finding type ID. It identifies the event's semantics and structure. The value is calculated by the logging system as: class_uid * 100 + activity_id.
type_uid.caption: Type ID

risk_score.description: The risk score as reported by the event source.
risk_score.caption: Risk Score

unmapped.description: The attributes that are not mapped to the event schema. The names and values of those attributes are specific to the event source.
unmapped.caption: Unmapped Data

authorizations.description: Provides details about an authorization, such as authorization outcome, and any associated policies related to the activity/event.
authorizations.caption: Authorization Information

confidence_score.description: The confidence score as reported by the event source.
confidence_score.caption: Confidence Score

end_time_dt.description: The end time of a time period, or the time of the most recent event included in the aggregate event.
end_time_dt.caption: End Time

observables.description: The observables associated with the event or a finding.
observables.caption: Observables

message.description: The description of the event/finding, as defined by the source.
message.caption: Message

activity_name.description: The event activity name, as defined by the activity_id.
activity_name.caption: Activity

is_alert.description: Indicates that the event is considered to be an alertable signal. Should be set to true if disposition_id = Alert among other dispositions, and/or risk_level_id or severity_id of the event is elevated. Not all control events will be alertable, for example if disposition_id = Exonerated or disposition_id = Allowed.
is_alert.caption: Alert

api.description: Describes details about a typical API (Application Programming Interface) call.
api.caption: API Details

category_name.description: The event category name, as defined by category_uid value: Application Activity.
category_name.caption: Category

policy.description: The policy that pertains to the control that triggered the event, if applicable. For example the name of an anti-malware policy or an access control policy.
policy.caption: Policy

confidence.description: The confidence, normalized to the caption of the confidence_id value. In the case of 'Other', it is defined by the event source.
confidence.caption: Confidence

count.description: The number of times that events in the same logical group occurred during the event Start Time to End Time period.
count.caption: Count

400405.description: DHCPACK: The server accepts the request by sending the client a DHCP Acknowledgment message.
400405.caption: DHCP Activity: Ack

400407.description: DHCPRELEASE: A DHCP client sends a DHCPRELEASE packet to the server to release the IP address and cancel any remaining lease.
400407.caption: DHCP Activity: Release

type_uid.description: The event/finding type ID. It identifies the event's semantics and structure. The value is calculated by the logging system as: class_uid * 100 + activity_id.
type_uid.caption: Type ID

relay.description: The network relay that is associated with the event.
relay.caption: Relay

proxy_traffic.description: The network traffic refers to the amount of data moving across a network, from proxy to remote server at a given point of time.
proxy_traffic.caption: Proxy Traffic

risk_score.description: The risk score as reported by the event source.
risk_score.caption: Risk Score

unmapped.description: The attributes that are not mapped to the event schema. The names and values of those attributes are specific to the event source.
unmapped.caption: Unmapped Data

authorizations.description: Provides details about an authorization, such as authorization outcome, and any associated policies related to the activity/event.
authorizations.caption: Authorization Information

confidence_score.description: The confidence score as reported by the event source.
confidence_score.caption: Confidence Score

end_time_dt.description: The end time of a time period, or the time of the most recent event included in the aggregate event.
end_time_dt.caption: End Time

ja4_fingerprint_list.description: A list of the JA4+ network fingerprints.
ja4_fingerprint_list.caption: JA4+ Fingerprints

observables.description: The observables associated with the event or a finding.
observables.caption: Observables

src_endpoint.description: The initiator (client) of the DHCP connection.
src_endpoint.caption: Source Endpoint

proxy_connection_info.description: The connection information from the proxy server to the remote server.
proxy_connection_info.caption: Proxy Connection Info

message.description: The description of the event/finding, as defined by the source.
message.caption: Message

packet_list.description: The list of packet objects describing captured network packets.
packet_list.caption: Packets

proxy_tls.description: The TLS protocol negotiated between the proxy server and the remote server.
proxy_tls.caption: Proxy TLS

activity_name.description: The event activity name, as defined by the activity_id.
activity_name.caption: Activity

is_alert.description: Indicates that the event is considered to be an alertable signal. Should be set to true if disposition_id = Alert among other dispositions, and/or risk_level_id or severity_id of the event is elevated. Not all control events will be alertable, for example if disposition_id = Exonerated or disposition_id = Allowed.
is_alert.caption: Alert

api.description: Describes details about a typical API (Application Programming Interface) call.
api.caption: API Details

category_name.description: The event category name, as defined by category_uid value: Network Activity.
category_name.caption: Category

policy.description: The policy that pertains to the control that triggered the event, if applicable. For example the name of an anti-malware policy or an access control policy.
policy.caption: Policy

confidence.description: The confidence, normalized to the caption of the confidence_id value. In the case of 'Other', it is defined by the event source.
confidence.caption: Confidence

count.description: The number of times that events in the same logical group occurred during the event Start Time to End Time period.
count.caption: Count

lease_dur.description: This represents the length of the DHCP lease in seconds. This is present in DHCP Ack events.
lease_dur.caption: Lease Duration

traffic.description: The network traffic for this observation period. Use when reporting: (1) delta values (bytes/packets transferred since the last observation), (2) instantaneous measurements at a specific point in time, or (3) standalone single-event metrics. This attribute represents a point-in-time measurement or incremental change, not a running total. For accumulated totals across multiple observations or the lifetime of a flow, use cumulative_traffic instead.
traffic.caption: Traffic

99.description: The risk level is not mapped. See the risk_level attribute, which contains a data source specific value.
99.caption: Other

end_time.description: The end time of a time period, or the time of the most recent event included in the aggregate event.
end_time.caption: End Time

proxy_http_response.description: The HTTP Response from the remote server to the proxy server.
proxy_http_response.caption: Proxy HTTP Response

enrichments.description:
The additional information from an external data source, which is associated with the event or a finding. For example add location information for the IP address in the DNS answers:

[{"name": "answers.ip", "value": "92.24.47.250", "type": "location", "data": {"city": "Socotra", "continent": "Asia", "coordinates": [-25.4153, 17.0743], "country": "YE", "desc": "Yemen"}}]
enrichments.caption: Enrichments

class_name.description: The event class name, as defined by class_uid value: DHCP Activity.
class_name.caption: Class

dst_endpoint.description: The responder (server) of the DHCP connection.
dst_endpoint.caption: Destination Endpoint

cumulative_traffic.description: The cumulative (running total) network traffic aggregated from the start of a flow or session. Use when reporting: (1) total accumulated bytes/packets since flow initiation, (2) combined aggregation models where both incremental deltas and running totals are reported together (populate both traffic for the delta and this attribute for the cumulative total), or (3) final summary metrics when a long-lived connection closes. This represents the sum of all activity from flow start to the current observation, not a delta or point-in-time value.
cumulative_traffic.caption: Cumulative Traffic

status_code.description:
The event status code, as reported by the event source.

For example, in a Windows Failed Authentication event, this would be the value of 'Failure Code', e.g. 0x18.
status_code.caption: Status Code

raw_data_size.description: The size of the raw data which was transformed into an OCSF event, in bytes.
raw_data_size.caption: Raw Data Size

app_name.description: The network application name identified by tools such as NBAR or App ID (e.g., youtube, facebook, webex). This represents a specific network application that uses standard protocols (such as https or quic) to deliver its service.
app_name.caption: Application Name

status.description: The event status, normalized to the caption of the status_id value. In the case of 'Other', it is defined by the event source.
status.caption: Status

raw_data.description: The raw event/finding data as received from the source.
raw_data.caption: Raw Data

99.description: The status is not mapped. See the status attribute, which contains a data source specific value.
99.caption: Other

status_id.description: The normalized identifier of the event status.
status_id.caption: Status ID

time_dt.description: The normalized event occurrence time or the finding creation time.
time_dt.caption: Event Time

status_detail.description: The status detail contains additional information about the event/finding outcome.
status_detail.caption: Status Detail

proxy.description: The proxy (server) in a network connection.
proxy.caption: Proxy

duration.description: The event duration or aggregate time, the amount of time the event covers from start_time to end_time in milliseconds.
duration.caption: Duration Milliseconds

severity.description: The event/finding severity, normalized to the caption of the severity_id value. In the case of 'Other', it is defined by the source.
severity.caption: Severity

start_time_dt.description: The start time of a time period, or the time of the least recent event included in the aggregate event.
start_time_dt.caption: Start Time

3.description: Action is required but the situation is not serious at this time.
3.caption: Medium

6.description: An error occurred but it is too late to take remedial action.
6.caption: Fatal

1.description: Informational message. No action required.
1.caption: Informational

2.description: The user decides if action is needed.
2.caption: Low
